page: Amazon Web Services g4dn.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive, network and EBS-optimized server instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores with hyper-threading operating at 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For hardware acceleration, it includes a single NVIDIA Turing T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 900 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear division between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. Single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng and single-threaded compression tests. However, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier. The standout capability of this instance is its machine learning performance; LLM inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation using small (135M) and medium (7B) models rank in the top tier (top 10%). For larger models like the 70B parameter variant, performance drops to average levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g4dn.8xlarge offers a balanced resource density, combining dedicated CPU resources, a mid-range GPU, and fast local NVMe storage without requiring the premium of high-end GPU architectures. This makes it highly suitable for workloads such as small-to-medium LLM inference, graphics-intensive applications, video transcoding, and machine learning model serving. It is also well-suited for database operations and general-purpose workloads that benefit from GPU acceleration and high network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.8xlarge is a GPU-accelerated, network-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 32 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a single NVIDIA Turing T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. Local storage is supported by a 900 GB NVMe SSD, complemented by a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. While single-core CPU benchmarks are weak, multi-core performance is average, and LLM inference for small and medium models ranks in the top tier. This instance provides a cost-effective balance of compute, memory, and graphics acceleration, making it suitable for machine learning inference, graphics-intensive applications, and database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.8xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, a 16 GB NVIDIA T4 GPU, and a 900 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for machine learning inference and graphics workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.8xlarge features 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, a 16 GB NVIDIA T4 GPU, and a 900 GB NVMe SSD for GPU-accelerated workloads.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance combining dedicated Intel Xeon processors, local NVMe storage, and high-speed networking for efficient machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Equipped with 32 vCPUs and 128 GB of DDR4 memory
Features a single NVIDIA Turing T4 GPU with 16 GB VRAM
Includes 900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance for small and medium LLM inference
Suited for graphics-intensive applications and ML model serving
